title: Continuous Delivery Outcomes, Flow, Feedback, and Evidence · Release Evidence Brief: Ci Value Stream Evidence Chain
summary: A civic TypeScript service ships manually, batches risky changes, measures only deployment count, and cannot connect a production incident to source, tests, artifact, approval, or rollback. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci value stream evidence chain increment in delivery outcome and evidence map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace requirement, source revision, review, checks, build inputs, immutable artifact, deployment, observation, and recovery evidence as one delivery chain.
Relate batch size, queue time, feedback latency, failure isolation, recovery cost, and learning speed without treating deployment frequency as the objective.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Ci Value Stream Evidence Chain · Theory Ci Value Stream Evidence Chain
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory service deploys 4 times per week but cannot connect reviewed source, required checks, immutable artifact, production revision, user signal, abort decision, or recovery. Define value and delivery evidence without gaming one metric.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Ci Value Stream Evidence Chain contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Ci Value Stream Evidence Chain · Theory Ci Value Stream Evidence Chain.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace requirement, source revision, review, checks, build inputs, immutable artifact, deployment, observation, and recovery evidence as one delivery chain. For Predict Ci Value Stream Evidence Chain · Theory Ci Value Stream Evidence Chain, keep Ci Value Stream Evidence Chain connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name event, workflow, revision, actor, permissions, runner, toolchain, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, user-signal, and recovery boundaries; predict every state and trust transition; review the smallest deterministic workflow or release change; then verify a fault, repair, rollback, and authorized live-system transfer limit. Apply that move specifically to Ci Value Stream Evidence Chain while producing delivery outcome and evidence map.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example service deploys 4 times per week but cannot connect reviewed source, required checks, immutable artifact, production revision, user signal, abort decision, or recovery. Define value and delivery evidence without gaming one metric. Competency probe: Trace requirement, source revision, review, checks, build inputs, immutable artifact, deployment, observation, and recovery evidence as one delivery chain. A defensible solution applies Ci Value Stream Evidence Chain, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to delivery outcome and evidence map.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-value-stream-evidence-chain — Trace requirement, source revision, review, checks, build inputs, immutable artifact, deployment, observation, and recovery evidence as one delivery chain.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-ci-delivery-outcomes-flow-evidence-4
  - competency: ci-worked-ci-delivery-outcomes-flow-evidence-4
    artifact: delivery-evidence.yaml
    decision: worked ci delivery outcomes flow evidence 4
    environment: GitHub-hosted ubuntu-24.04 runner on a cold cache
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      hypothesis: safer-status-release
      source-revision: reviewed-sha
      checks: required-and-observed
      artifact: immutable-digest
      deployment: revision-identity
      user-signal: successful-status-read
      abort-signal: error-budget-breach
      recovery: prior-digest
    verify: review source-to-user evidence chain and calculate lead time failure and recovery boundaries
    failure: Reject deployment count as value, green checks as provenance, or pipeline completion as user outcome.
    repair: Link reviewed source, exact checks, immutable artifact, observed deployment, user signal, abort decision, and recovery evidence.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A green workflow run proves the deployed artifact came from the reviewed source. This fails the Ci Value Stream Evidence Chain contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Ci Value Stream Evidence Chain evidence · Theory Ci Value Stream Evidence Chain
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-value-stream-evidence-chain-ohk8mn". Tailor its delivery-evidence.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

    artifact: delivery-evidence.yaml
    decision: value stream evidence chain

    retained-proof: lock hash cold install test report package contents and output digest

    verify: review source-to-user evidence chain and calculate lead time failure and recovery boundaries
    failure: Reject deployment count as value, green checks as provenance, or pipeline completion as user outcome.
    repair: Link reviewed source, exact checks, immutable artifact, observed deployment, user signal, abort decision, and recovery evidence.
